title: chenopodium subficifolium
intro: Chenopodium subficifolium, also known as narrow-leaved lamb's quarters, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is a type of succulent herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Chenopodium subficifolium can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, offering potential health benefits including relief from fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
